edge_hypothesis: T3_10_0.7 and ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 are both computed every bar but NEVER tested in any strategy. Both are designed to reduce lag vs standard EMAs — T3 via triple smoothing, ALMA via Gaussian weighting. When two lag-reduced MAs cross, the signal should detect trend changes EARLIER than EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover, with fewer false signals because both indicators have built-in noise filtering. ema_crossover has 0 ACCEPTs ever — this tests whether the problem was the EMA math (too laggy, too noisy) rather than the crossover concept itself. CHOP gate filters deep-range noise where crossovers whipsaw.
target_regime: transitional (cross fires when trend is forming — CHOP < 61.8 confirms market leaving range) + trending continuation

variants.name: t3_alma_crossover_trend_8to1
variants.description: T3 crosses ALMA with loose CHOP gate (< 61.8) at 8:1 R:R. Tests whether lag-reduced crossovers generate viable signals where standard EMA crossovers failed. CHOP < 61.8 allows both trending and transitional entries.

components.notes: T3_10_0.7 — triple-smoothed EMA, low lag trend direction. NEVER TESTED.
ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 — Gaussian-weighted MA, lag-reduced crossover partner. NEVER TESTED.

variants.name: t3_alma_crossover_transition_10to1
variants.description: T3 crosses ALMA with tight CHOP gate (< 50) at 10:1 R:R matching Vortex v3a geometry. CHOP < 50 means market is actively trending or just entered a trend — targets transition/trending entries only. Tighter stop (1.0 ATR) because signals at CHOP < 50 have stronger conviction.

components.notes: T3_10_0.7 — triple-smoothed EMA, low lag trend direction
CHOP_14_1_100 < 50 selects strong trending/transitional bars only
